name.es: Bath bun (bollo inglés con azúcar en grano)
name.fr: Bath bun (petit pain anglais au sucre en grains)

description.en: A rich, brioche-like bun from the English spa town of Bath, studded with currants and candied peel and topped with coarse nib sugar for crunch. An 18th-century tea-time favorite, said to be beloved by Jane Austen.
description.de: Ein reichhaltiges, brioche-artiges Brötchen aus der englischen Kurstadt Bath, gespickt mit Korinthen und kandierter Schale und mit grobem Hagelzucker für den Knusper bestreut. Ein Teestunden-Favorit des 18. Jahrhunderts, angeblich von Jane Austen geliebt.
description.es: Un bollo rico, parecido a la brioche, de la ciudad balneario inglesa de Bath, con grosellas y cáscara confitada y cubierto de azúcar en grano grueso para el crujido. Un favorito de la hora del té del siglo XVIII, que se dice que encantaba a Jane Austen.
description.fr: Un petit pain riche, façon brioche, de la ville thermale anglaise de Bath, parsemé de raisins de Corinthe et d'écorces confites et couronné de gros sucre en grains pour le croquant. Un favori de l'heure du thé au XVIIIe siècle, dit-on apprécié de Jane Austen.

origin.note: Credited to 18th-century Bath baker William Oliver, of Bath Oliver biscuit fame. Originally topped with sugar-coated caraway comfits, later simplified to caraway seeds or, as here, coarse nib sugar.

ingredients.note: beaten, for egg wash before baking
coarse nib or pearl sugar, sprinkled on top before baking

keeping.note: Best fresh the day they're baked, while the nib sugar still crunches; day-old buns are good split and toasted.

method.title: Rub in butter
method.summary: Sift the flour with the salt, then rub in the butter until the mixture looks like coarse crumbs.

method.title: Bloom & mix
method.summary: Whisk the warm milk with the yeast and a pinch of the sugar until frothy, then beat in the egg, lemon zest and remaining sugar, and combine with the flour mixture.

method.title: Knead & first rise
method.summary: Knead into a smooth, elastic dough for about 10 minutes, then cover and prove in a warm place until doubled, about 45 minutes.

method.title: Add fruit
method.summary: Knead in the currants and candied orange peel just until distributed.

method.title: Shape & short second rise
method.summary: Divide into 4 balls, space on a greased tray, and let puff for about 10 minutes.

method.title: Glaze, top & bake
method.summary: Brush with beaten egg, scatter with coarse nib sugar, and bake until golden.

source.adaptation: formula.* covers the dough only; egg wash and nib-sugar topping are excluded. Source's '1/2 oz active yeast' recorded as fresh yeast; ounce/cup measures converted to grams via standard conversions.
